ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Pudina Paneer Tikka. Mapped to the closest_parent 'paneer (food product)' as the closest defensible FoodOn anchor. The tikka preparation and mint marinade are not captured at this level.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cheese, fresh (unripened) — used as the closest FoodEx2 anchor for paneer, which is a fresh unripened cheese. The grilled tikka preparation and mint marinade are not captured at this level.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for paneer tikka or pudina paneer tikka. A0CKP (fresh unripened cheese) is the closest_parent defensible FoodEx2 anchor for the primary ingredient.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for paneer tikka or pudina paneer tikka. SNOMED CT does not have sufficient granularity for this prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical restaurant serving of Pudina Paneer Tikka consists of approximately 4 to 5 pieces of grilled paneer, weighing around 200 g in total, served with mint chutney and sliced onions.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — Paneer (Cottage Cheese) and Tandoori Paneer Tikka composition data
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition) provides composition data for paneer (raw) and for tandoori paneer preparations. Values for Pudina Paneer Tikka are adapted from the ICMR-NIN paneer tikka entry, adjusted for the mint-coriander marinade and tandoor grilling method. The mint and coriander marinade contributes negligible macronutrient change relative to plain paneer tikka. Sodium is elevated relative to raw paneer due to marinade spices and salt. Values represent a composite estimate grounded in ICMR-NIN primary data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Cheese and high-protein low-carbohydrate foods; paneer and analogous fresh cheese entries
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Pudina Paneer Tikka in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI is estimated at 27 based on the analogous entry for paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) and similar high-protein, low-carbohydrate grilled cheese preparations in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) international GI tables. The low carbohydrate content (4.2 g per 100 g) and high protein and fat content of paneer tikka strongly suppress postprandial glucose response, consistent with a GI in the low range (below 30). Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (27 × 4.2 × 2) / 100 = 2.3 g, classified as low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from marinade spices and salt
hypertension.guidance: Pudina Paneer Tikka contains approximately 380 mg sodium per 100 g (760 mg per 200 g serving), which represents about one-third of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals; portion control and requesting reduced-salt preparation are advisable for those managing blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and low carbohydrate content
type2_diabetes.guidance: Pudina Paneer Tikka has a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 27 and a glycemic load of only 2.3 per standard serving, making it a suitable high-protein, low-GI option for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed without high-carbohydrate accompaniments such as naan or rice.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat content from full-fat paneer
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Each 200 g serving of Pudina Paneer Tikka provides approximately 17.6 g of saturated fat, which exceeds the recommended daily limit of 14–20 g for cardiovascular risk management; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and frequency of consumption, and prefer lower-fat paneer preparations where available.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pudina Paneer Tikka is a popular North Indian vegetarian grilled dish consisting of cubes of homemade paneer (fresh Indian cottage cheese) marinated in a vibrant green paste of fresh mint (pudina), coriander leaves, yogurt, ginger, garlic, green chilli, cumin, garam masala, lemon juice, and salt, then skewered and cooked in a traditional tandoor (clay oven) at high heat. The result is a dish with a lightly charred, aromatic exterior and a soft, creamy interior. The mint and coriander marinade imparts a distinctly fresh, herbaceous, and mildly spiced flavour profile that differentiates it from the more common red-marinated paneer tikka. Pudina Paneer Tikka is served as a starter or appetiser in North Indian restaurants across India and in the Indian diaspora restaurant scene in Singapore, where it appears on menus at establishments such as Curry Village, Bawarchi, Bombay Grill, Bombay Curry and Kebabs, and Aryana. Nutritionally, it is a high-protein, moderate-fat, low-carbohydrate dish. Per 100 g it provides approximately 197 kcal, 13.5 g protein, 14.2 g fat (of which 8.8 g is saturated), and only 4.2 g carbohydrate, making it one of the lower-glycemic-index options in Indian restaurant menus. The dish is a good source of calcium (approximately 208 mg per 100 g) and vitamin B12 from the paneer. It is naturally gluten-free and suitable for vegetarians and halal diners. Clinically, its low GI and high protein content make it appropriate for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, though its saturated fat and sodium content warrant moderation for those with cardiovascular disease or hypertension.
common_user_queries: Is paneer tikka good for diabetics?
What is the difference between pudina paneer tikka and regular paneer tikka?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN primary data for paneer and tandoori paneer tikka preparations. No exact ICMR-NIN entry for Pudina Paneer Tikka exists; values are grounded in the closest ICMR-NIN paneer tikka composition data with minor adjustments for the mint-coriander marinade. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on paneer and similar high-protein low-carbohydrate grilled cheese foods. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) paneer and paneer tikka entries. No exact published composition entry for Pudina Paneer Tikka was found in any approved primary source. 2. GI of 27 is an analogous estimate based on paneer and similar fresh cheese entries in the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this dish was found. 3. Sodium value (380 mg per 100 g) reflects a typical restaurant marinade with salt; home-prepared versions may have lower sodium. 4. The dish is served at 5 field survey data restaurants in the price range SGD 16.90–17.90 per portion. 5. FoodOn and FoodEx2 codes are mapped to the closest_parent paneer/fresh cheese category as no specific ontology code for paneer tikka exists in either system. 6. Vitamin D data not available from ICMR-NIN for this preparation; reported as -1. 7. Selenium data not available; reported as -1.
